pages.full_text: We had lunch in town, pork chops, & then drove about 17 miles to S. Pedro, a C- & church, Quite like St. Peter's in Cambridge. It has the crispest Romanesque capitols that I've ever seen - Daniel in the Den + Isaac sacrificing Esau. It had been moved up, stone by stone, to a higher village in 1930's when they flooded the valleys. Also flooded were the ravines which Wellington crossed
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in colour postcard of San Pedro de la Nave church captioned 'San Pedro de la Nave'. Text continues around the postcard. 'C- & church' likely abbreviation for C of E or similar; may be 'C-& church' meaning a Visigothic or Romanesque church. 'capitols' written as-is (likely meaning 'capitals' i.e. carved column capitals). The text at the bottom is cut off at the gutter/page edge — continues on the right page.

pages.full_text:
when he evaded the French Blockade by going through rough country NW of Zamora. He was pulled across the ravines in a hammock.
	We returned to Zamora & had supper on benches on a little platform overlooking the river. An amazing number of Storks flew past & there were 25 on the finials of the cathedral & the tower.
	At 8.30 we joined a group to look at the Co olive mills, 3 of them, by the river. They've restored the mill wheel & hammer very well.
__Friday August 1st!__
	We set off for Salamanca & parked across the river. We saw the Patio de Escuelas, with the amazing plateresque carved front to the university building, with the symbol for lust - a frog on a skull (see over). We saw the house of shells, & inside it to a lovely courtyard. The old & new cathedrals are beautiful - domes & 1492 [illegible] [illegible]
pages.notes: 'Friday August 1st' is underlined; Friday 1 August fell in 2008 within the volume's date range (2003–2019). 'Co olive mills' — 'Co' may be an abbreviation (possibly 'Roman'). 'finials' — written as 'ginials' in the diary but likely means finials. Last two words at page bottom are cut off and illegible. The page number '2' appears at the top right corner.
